title: AEP v1.2 F21 Claim Enemy Pairing Record
description: v1.2 immune-system primitive. Every PROVEN/RELIABLE claim ships with its strongest plausible enemy: the condition under which the claim would be false (operator source.md L154-164). Operator's framing: 'most AI systems only optimize for saying true-looking things. AEP should force every claim to carry its own assassin.' Lineage NOVEL: no direct external precedent (closest is Popperian falsificationism + Toulmin argument structure rebuttal field, both philosophical not structural). HV2 closure: enemy_authored_by_principal_id != claim_authored_by_principal_id HARD-CONSTRAINED via schema; enemy_review_required_by_role enum HARD-CONSTRAINED to [judge, adversary]; enemy_basis_source_ids MUST include >=1 source NOT in claim.basis_source_ids[]. Composes_with: v1.1 F13 ClaimRuntimeFalsifier (the enemy IS the falsifier in claim_enemy_pairing form).

bound_to_claim_id.description: Claim ID this enemy pairs against. Must resolve to an entry in data/claims.jsonl.

claim_authored_by_principal_id.description: Principal ID of the claim author. HV2 closure: enemy.authored_by_principal_id MUST differ from this.

claim_truth_tag.description: Enforcement scope (plan sec6 + operator L154 + adversary A2 closure): enemy pairing REQUIRED on PROVEN/RELIABLE claims; advisory on STRONGLY PLAUSIBLE; not required on lower tiers (to prevent enforcement scope creep).

enemy_text.description: Operator L156 verbatim. The strongest plausible condition under which the claim would be false. Plain language; civilian-readable. Operator L159-162 example: claim='The agent reviewed all expected packets'; enemy='One expected packet was omitted from expected_corpus_scope or touched only by metadata, not content'.

enemy_authored_by_principal_id.description: HV2 closure HARD-CONSTRAINED: this MUST differ from claim_authored_by_principal_id. Schema-level enforcement via not-equal assertion in validator (additionalProperties:false plus runtime check). Adversary attack A2 mitigation: enemy text from the same principal is sycophantic-tautology-shaped per Toulmin rebuttal discipline.

enemy_authored_by_role.description: HV2 closure HARD-CONSTRAINED. Only judge or adversary may author an enemy pairing on a PROVEN/RELIABLE claim. Scribe + curator + forge + pathfinder + strategist + scout + warden + visual-judge are explicitly EXCLUDED from enemy authorship to enforce independence.

enemy_review_required_by_role.description: HV2 closure HARD-CONSTRAINED. The role(s) required to attest the enemy pairing was peer-reviewed. At minimum one of {judge, adversary} must sign the review attestation.

enemy_basis_source_ids.description: Adversary A2 cheapest disconfirmer: enemy MUST cite different evidence than the claim. Validator enforces: at least one source in enemy_basis_source_ids[] is NOT in claim.basis_source_ids[] at pairing time. Hard fail if enemy_basis_source_ids is a subset of claim.basis_source_ids.

claim_basis_source_ids_at_pairing_time.description: Snapshot of claim.basis_source_ids at the time the enemy was paired. Used to compute the source-divergence invariant. If empty, enemy_basis_source_ids must be non-empty and the validator emits AEP12_F21_CLAIM_BASIS_EMPTY warning.

falsifier_id.description: Bound to v1.1 F13 ClaimRuntimeFalsifier id.

executable_cmd.description: Operator L163 verbatim example: 'prove packet content access, not just filename touch.' The enemy MUST be testable; the falsifier is the test. F13 anti-tautology reuse per A2 closure (do not invent new layer per sec50 EH Law-3).

expected_exit.description: 0 = enemy disproven (claim survives); 1 = enemy confirmed (claim falsified).

method.description: Adversary A4 closure: anti-tautology stops at depth 2 — reuse F13 anti-tautology + F14 rater quorum, do NOT invent meta-adversary.

token_overlap_ratio.description: If method == automated_token_overlap_check, the token overlap between claim_text and enemy_text. Threshold 0.5 = boilerplate; 0.8 = tautology.

pairing_signature_ed25519.description: Ed25519 signature freezing this pairing's bytes at pairing time. Composes with v0.8 F3.

examples.enemy_text: The PROV round-trip test fixture imported via the same library that emitted the export; an external W3C PROV validator (rdflib + prov-toolbox) was never invoked. The claim 'AEP packet round-trips against PROV' is true ONLY against the in-repo validator, not the canonical external validator.

required_falsifier.executable_cmd: python tools/run_external_prov_validator.py --packet $PACKET --validator prov-toolbox && python tools/run_external_prov_validator.py --packet $PACKET --validator rdflib
